description: Go constant-time analysis benchmark corpus. Mirror of the C corpus (15 items in benchmark/corpus/). Items labeled clean (designed CT, expect 0 errors after secret-aware filtering), vulnerable (real timing leak, expect detection), or limitation_no_signal (real leak that instruction-level analysis cannot detect). Each item lives in its own directory with a go.mod so it can be built standalone.

items.label: clean
items.notes: ConstantTimeCompare loops len(x) times unconditionally, accumulating XOR via OR. Loop bound is the public LENGTH, not the content. Final fold uses (x-1)>>31 -- bit-twiddling, no branches.

items.label: clean
items.notes: Branchless bitmask blending. The (v-1) trick yields all-ones / all-zeros without a conditional move.

items.source: ChaCha20 quarter-round (DJB reference, mirrored in Go's chacha20)
items.label: clean
items.notes: ARX-only: add, rotate, XOR. Loop counters are public constants (10 double-rounds, 16 state words).

items.label: clean
items.notes: Output-length loop bound is public. HMAC body would be a sub-call below the source-level analyzer's view.

items.label: clean
items.notes: uint64 multiplies + carry chain via shift+mask. cswap uses XOR blending: both halves always execute.

items.label: clean
items.notes: The mod-by-blockSize is on a public counter. The block cipher itself is a sub-call below scope.

items.source: Barrett reduction for ML-KEM (CIRCL pke/kyber/internal/common style)
items.label: clean
items.notes: Replaces `x % Q` with multiply-by-magic; no DIV in the disassembly. Same for Montgomery reduce.

items.label: clean
items.notes: Buffer-cursor branch (idx==64) is on a public counter. Compression ARX has no data-dependent branches.

items.label: vulnerable
items.notes: Integer divide on secret coefficient with a runtime-loaded modulus (compile-time-constant divisors get folded by Go's SSA into multiply-by-magic, accidentally CT-fixing the bug). L26 = compress (q=num/Q; Go fuses the subsequent x%Q into the same IDIV). L28 = branch on secret-derived r. L35 = reduce.

items.label: vulnerable
items.notes: L18 = loop bound on secret padLen. L19 = early-exit on padding-byte mismatch. L32 = early-exit byte compare in verifyMAC (variable-time MAC compare).

items.source: Naive RSA modexp (Kocher 1996 timing attack pattern)
items.label: vulnerable
items.notes: L16 = secret-bit branch (`exp & 1 == 1`). L17 + L20 = `% m` on secret-derived intermediates.

items.source: Naive MAC compare via bytes.Equal + unrolled loop variant
items.label: vulnerable
items.notes: L18 = bytes.Equal on secret-named MAC (variable-time in Go despite popular belief; runtime.memequal_varlen has early exit). L29 = early-exit in unrolled-loop variant. The bytes.Equal case requires the source-level memcmp-source filter to recognize.

items.label: vulnerable
items.notes: Equality on fixed-size byte arrays. The compiler emits inlined byte compare or runtime.memequal; either way it is a memcmp-on-secret pattern at the source level. The source-level filter recognises it via the secret-named parameter.

items.label: limitation_no_signal
items.notes: Real cache-timing leak: Te0[secretIdx] reads a different cache line per byte of secret state. Instruction-level analysis cannot see this -- there is no DIV, no branch on a secret bit. Documents the analyzer's blind spot.
